title: Single-character alternations in regular expressions should be replaced with character classes
description: Single-character alternations in regular expressions should be replaced with character classesAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode Smellregextype-dependentWhy is this an issue?When an alternation contains multiple alternatives that consist of a single character, it can be rewritten as a character class. This should be preferred because it is more efficient and can even help prevent stack overflows when used inside a repetition (see ruleS5998).Noncompliant code example/a|b|c/; // NoncompliantCompliant solution/[abc]/; // or /[a-c]/;Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionA
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Replace single character alternations in regex literals with character classes
Replace single character alternations in groups with character classes
Replace single character alternations in RegExp constructor with character classes
Replace single character alternations in regex methods with character classes
Replace single character alternations in string patterns with character classes
